description:
Post-finalization spec revision 2026-06-05 (bl-2e26 §6 'bl install' block). Amends landed bl-66e7 ('bl install — committed-subtree transfer', commit 03fe1ed) — assume it landed; this corrects it.

WHY: install is now the SOLE consent-gated config transport (no shadowing). It must be destructive-of-the-named-class but never able to eat a co-resident store.

WHAT (src/install.rs):
- Scope is a PATH under config/ (was a flat {plugins,config,tasks} enum): 'bl install plugins' wipes ALL plugin wiring + copies source's; 'bl install plugins/tracker' wipes just that subtree; 'bl install config' replaces config/balls.toml. Naming a parent replaces everything under it; a child replaces only that child. Delete-then-copy of exactly the named class — never merge.
- THE INVARIANT: install builds its commit on top of --to's CURRENT tip, swapping only <scope>; it NEVER rebuilds the tip from the source's whole tree and NEVER resets the ref. This keeps siblings (esp. a co-resident tasks/ when tasks_branch points at the same branch, §2) untouched — a whole-tree replace / ref reset is FORBIDDEN, not just discouraged.
- 'tasks' is the one MERGE scope and a DIFFERENT branch (not config): union-merge two store branches (same-id = conflict), the one-time store-RELOCATE primitive. (copy_wiring currently adds symlinks without pruning stale ones — must become wipe-scope-then-copy.)
- RETIRE: the 'terminus' pointer-name default and the 'next:' pointer-exclusion clause (no trail). Default --to landing, --from the configured upstream/any ref.
- Keep: committed-subtree-only (never bin/), local binary resolution + protocol validation.
title: Greenfield §6: install becomes scope-addressed destructive-replace (subtree-only-rewrite invariant); tasks=merge exception
